attributes.io.input: {"wing":"reference","room":"nextjs","content":"In Next.js 16 canary, `cacheComponents: true` is a **top-level** config key in `next.config.ts`, NOT under `experimental`. Placing it under `experimental` emits a deprecation warning but still works.","importance":2,"source":"build-error debugging during admin products page implementation"}
attributes.io.output: {"drawer_id":"drawer_reference_nextjs_b2ea77de","lock_wait_ms":0}

attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.input: {"wing":"reference","room":"nextjs","content":"In Next.js 16 canary, `revalidateTag(tag)` now requires a second argument (`profile: string | CacheLifeConfig`). Omitting it prints a deprecation warning. For Server Actions, use `updateTag(tag)` instead — it only takes the tag string, provides read-your-own-writes semantics, and triggers immediate expiration without needing a cache profile.","importance":2,"source":"TypeScript compilation error during admin products page implementation"}
attributes.io.output: {"drawer_id":"drawer_reference_nextjs_e88b29d3","lock_wait_ms":0}

attributes.gen_ai.operation.name: execute_tool
attributes.io.input: {"wing":"reference","room":"caching-patterns","content":"For read-heavy admin catalog pages, use the `\"use cache\"` directive with `cacheTag(\"products\")` and `cacheLife({ stale: N, revalidate: N })` to serve cached data without re-querying on every request. Trigger cache invalidation via background sync: fire-and-forget a sync promise in a Server Action, call `updateTag(\"products\")` in the `.then()` after sync completes. The admin form returns immediately (stale data served briefly), and the next page view picks up fresh data once the cache tag is invalidated. This gives eventual consistency across views that share the same tag.","importance":3,"source":"admin product catalog page implementation"}
attributes.io.output: {"drawer_id":"drawer_reference_caching_patterns_696f4342","lock_wait_ms":0}
